data.title: California American Water restores key water well after 2024 storm damage, completes infrastructure upgrades in Monterey Peninsula.
data.description: In Pacific Grove, California, California American Water has successfully brought a crucial water well back online after extensive repairs and upgrades in its Monterey Peninsula water system. Following damage caused by two large eucalyptus trees during the 2024 winter storms, the Pearce well was taken out of service, impacting a significant water supply source. The repair work included replacing the well head, submersible pump, and electrical equipment. This infrastructure project was completed alongside the return of the well to full functionality.

data.title: Construction Specialties appoints Nick Longman as new CEO, leading global building products manufacturing company.
data.description: Nick Longman has been appointed as the Chief Executive Officer of Construction Specialties, a renowned global leader in building products manufacturing based in Lebanon, New Jersey.

data.title: In August, 22,000 jobs were added. Revised data reveals a 13,000 job loss in June, the first since Dec 2020.
data.description: In August, employers increased their workforce by 22,000 jobs. However, revised figures revealed a decrease of 13,000 jobs in June, marking the first overall decline in employment since December 2020.

data.title: Jobless rate falls unexpectedly, impacting Fed rate cut odds; S&P 500 rises.
data.description: The unemployment rate was projected to reach its highest point since October 2021. As per the latest jobs report, there has been a decrease in the chances of a Federal Reserve rate cut, indicating a rise in the unemployment rate. Consequently, the S&P 500 saw an increase in its value.

data.title: APFC Board greenlights FY25 financials, proceeds with FY27 budget proposal. Key focus on talent, security, investments.
data.description: The Alaska Permanent Fund Corporation (APFC) Board of Trustees met in Juneau to discuss financial matters on September 4th. The meeting included a review and approval of the annual audited financial statements. The board also advanced the budget proposal for Fiscal Year 2027 to support key areas such as talent, security, and investments. Additionally, the Ethics, Audit & Cybersecurity Committee met to examine the external audit findings from KPMG and to address issues concerning cybersecurity and ethics. The focus was on ensuring the proper handling of funds and maintaining security protocols.

data.title: XRP ecosystem expands to Harvard hackathon with 200 developers and $35,000 prize pool focusing on cross-chain development.
data.description: This month, the XRP ecosystem, including Flare, XRPL Commons, and EasyA, will showcase its capabilities at Harvard University with a 36-hour hackathon on cross-chain development on September 20–21. The event aims to attract 200 developers competing for a total prize pool of $35,000. Emphasizing the support for decentralized finance, payments, and consumer services, organizers plan to demonstrate the potential of XRP Ledger and Flare's interoperability protocols. This initiative is part of XRPFi ecosystem's efforts to engage with the academic and developer communities. The competition details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TVCMALL continues to empower retailers at IFA 2025 with one-stop wholesale solutions, with 17 years of experience and over 30 leading European retail partners.
data.description: Berlin is hosting IFA 2025, emphasizing smart innovation, connected living, and sustainable tech. TVCMALL is making its third appearance at Hall 3.2, Booth 121 (H3.2–121), highlighting its status as the top wholesaler for European mobile accessories retailers. With 17 years in the industry, TVCMALL has become the preferred supply chain partner for over 30 major European retailers.
